methodsAttempted.query: site:ksu.edu.sa King Saud University generative AI policy students academic integrity artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official KSU PDFs including the AI Office education guideline and the Student Learning Assessment Policy, plus lower-specificity journal and research results.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ksu.edu.sa "Guidelines for the Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence" "King Saud University"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located an official KSU-hosted PDF that referenced the university generative AI education guideline.
Arabic discovery found the official AI Office guidelines page and several training, news, and college/faculty-specific pages that were not used for broad claims.
Robots.txt allowed public access to the retrieved official AI Office and Center for Excellence in Learning and Teaching files; no login, CAPTCHA, or WAF bypass was attempted.

artifacts.summary: Official-source-only discovery found King Saud University AI Office generative AI education guidelines and the 2025 Student Learning Assessment Policy. Lower-specificity official journal, college-news, and training/login-style pages were recorded as rejected and were not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official KSU AI Office page listing the generative AI in education guideline PDF and update date.

artifacts.claimText: King Saud University's AI Office guideline is scoped to generative AI use in teaching and learning by students and faculty, not to all university personnel or general technical AI uses.
artifacts.normalizedValue: education-only scope for students and faculty

citation.citationTitle: دليل إرشادات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في التعليم للطلاب وأعضاء هيئة التدريس بجامعة الملك سعود
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Students may not submit content generated with generative AI as their own without disclosure, and using AI in exams, assignments, or academic assessments is treated as cheating unless the course instructor permits it.
artifacts.normalizedValue: disclosure required; unauthorized assessment use treated as cheating

citation.citationTitle: دليل إرشادات استخدام الذكاء الاصطناعي التوليدي في التعليم للطلاب وأعضاء هيئة التدريس بجامعة الملك سعود
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Students may not use generative AI tools to process confidential King Saud University information, such as student records or internal policies, without official university authorization.
artifacts.normalizedValue: no confidential KSU data in generative AI tools without authorization

artifacts.sourceTitle: Student Learning Assessment Policy at King Saud University 2025/1447AH
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The university supports the use of these tools, emphasizing: Submitted assignments and tasks must contain the student's original work. The student must disclose the use of generative AI (where and how it was used). Faculty members... have the right to determine which assignments a student is permitted and prohibited from using generative AI tools based on the course objectives.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The assessment policy supports generative AI tools while emphasizing original work, disclosure of AI use, and faculty authority to permit or prohibit use by assignment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 11: Using digital technology in assessment
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a scope/source-status claim limited to the AI Office guideline itself.
Approved because the Arabic guideline directly prohibits undisclosed AI-generated submissions and unauthorized assessment use.
Approved because the Arabic guideline directly prohibits processing confidential KSU information without official authorization.
Approved because the Arabic guideline directly requires faculty to state course AI-use and disclosure policy.
Approved with wording limited to the 2025 Student Learning Assessment Policy section on generative AI in assessment.

artifacts.title: King Saud University generative AI education and assessment guidance staged review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official King Saud University AI Office Arabic guidance for generative AI use in teaching and learning by students and faculty, plus an official 2025 Student Learning Assessment Policy section on generative AI. Approved claims are limited to source scope, student disclosure and unauthorized assessment use, confidential-information handling, faculty course-policy disclosure, and assessment-policy treatment of generative AI.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

Approved evidence is limited to public official KSU sources fetched during this run; college-specific news, journal publication instructions, and login-oriented training pages were not used for broad claims.
The Arabic AI Office guideline states it covers students and faculty in teaching and learning only; no university-wide claim is made for all staff or all technical AI uses.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
